title: Virtual speak
prompt_md:
## What to build

Making a base-class function `virtual` switches dispatch from compile time to runtime, so a call through a base pointer or reference runs the *derived* version. Build an `Animal` base class whose `speak()` is `virtual` and returns `"..."`, with derived classes `Cat` (`"Meow"`), `Dog` (`"Woof"`), and `Cow` (`"Moo"`).

Read the animals into a collection of `Animal*` (base pointers). Print each animal's sound by calling `speak()` through the base pointer, then print a final `Chorus:` line listing every sound separated by single spaces.

## Input

- Line 1: integer `N` (1 <= N <= 20).
- Next `N` lines: one of `cat`, `dog`, `cow`.

## Output

`N` lines, each the sound of the corresponding animal (via the base pointer), then a final line:
```
Chorus: <sound1> <sound2> ... <soundN>
```

test_cases.label: One of each
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Meow
Woof
Moo
Chorus: Meow Woof Moo

test_cases.label: Five mixed animals
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Moo
Moo
Meow
Woof
Meow
Chorus: Moo Moo Meow Woof Meow

title: Polymorphic shape areas
prompt_md:
## What to build

Use virtual dispatch to total the area of a mixed collection of shapes. Build a base class `Shape` with a `virtual double area() const` returning `0.0`, and derived classes `Circle` (area `pi * r * r`), `Rectangle` (area `w * h`), and `Triangle` (area `0.5 * base * height`). The constant `pi = 3.14159265` is provided in the starter.

Store the shapes as base pointers, then loop once computing the running total area and tracking the single largest area, calling `area()` through the base pointer each time.

## Input

- Line 1: integer `N` (1 <= N <= 20).
- Next `N` lines: `circle <r>`, `rect <w> <h>`, or `tri <base> <height>` (all positive integers).

## Output

Exactly two lines, each value truncated to an integer with `static_cast<int>`:
```
Total: <sum of all areas>
Largest: <largest single area>
```
